properties.description: a stone’s throw from the vibrant, historic heart of Totnes High Street, this stunning first-floor apartment ...Listed on 23rd Jun 2026
properties.fullDescription: Positioned just a stone’s throw from the vibrant, historic heart of Totnes High Street, this stunning first-floor apartment offers the ultimate blend of town-centre convenience and characterful charm.Combining unique period-style features with a bright, modern layout, this property represents a rare find in the local market. Complete with its own private entrance, allocated parking, and a shared outside space, it serves as the perfect starter home or an idyllic Devon bolthole.You are quite literally seconds away from Totnes' famous independent boutiques, artisan coffee shops, organic eateries, and weekly markets.With the River Dart nearby and excellent transport links (including the mainline train station), this location perfectly encapsulates the bohemian, community-driven lifestyle that makes Totnes so highly sought after.Property DescriptionThe Entrance: Leaving the hustle and bustle of Fore Street behind, you are welcomed by your own private entrance. A practical porch area offers the perfect spot to kick off shoes and hang coats before stepping into the main living space.The Living Space: The heart of this home is the beautiful open-plan lounge and dining area. Flooded with natural light, this space sets a warm and inviting tone, anchoring the apartment’s character with gorgeous parquet flooring and a cozy log burner-ideal for chilly Devon evenings.The Kitchen: The living area flows seamlessly into a smartly designed galley kitchen. Striking the perfect balance between form and function, it maximizes space and keeps the chef of the house fully connected to the social hub of the home.Bedrooms & Bathroom: An inner hallway, complete with handy built-in storage, guides you to the rest of the accommodation. Here, you will find two comfortable double bedrooms, both offering peaceful retreats at the end of the day, alongside a well-appointed family bathroom.Properties this close to the center of Totnes rarely come with external perks, but this apartment delivers:Allocated Parking: Forget the stress of town-centre parking with your own designated space.Shared Outside Space: A lovely communal area to enjoy a morning coffee or a breath of fresh air.Storage Shed: Excellent additional space for bikes, tools, or outdoor gear.Property Ownership InformationTenureLeaseholdCouncil Tax BandBAnnual Ground RentNo ground rentGround Rent Review PeriodNo review periodAnnual Service ChargeNo service chargeService Charge Review PeriodNo review periodLease End Date11/11/2976Property Description DisclaimerThis is a general description of the property only, and is not intended to constitute part of an offer or contract. properties.title: 2 bed property for sale
properties.fullDescription: SituationThe unique and charming market town of Totnes sits above the River Dart in the heart of the South Hams. The town is one of Devon's gems, the birthplace of the Transition Town movement in England and nominated in British Airways' HighLife magazine as one of the world's top ten funky towns. Full of colour and character, its atmosphere is cosmopolitan with a West Country flavour and those looking for history, alternative therapies and beautiful countryside will find it in and around Totnes. The town is said to have more listed buildings per head of the population than anywhere else in Britain. With a twice-a-week market and full of small independent shops, it's well-known for ethical products, wholefoods and fair trade goods. It is the second oldest borough in England, with an ancient Guildhall and Church, a Norman Castle and its North and East Gates. There is a well-regarded community college and a mainline railway station and Dartmoor, the South Hams and many of its fine beaches are within easy reach.DescriptionA ground floor purpose built retirement flat, which is part of the Manor House development, within a short, level walk of the town centre and shops. The accommodation includes a living room which overlooks the communal courtyard garden, a well maintained kitchen, two bedrooms and a shower room.The current owners have replaced the kitchen, bathroom and heating system in their ownership.TenureLeasehold.Lease has been extended until 2127.Ground rent payable March and September £76.96 [£152,92 pa]. Service charge payable March and September £1,746.99 [£3,494.00 pa].The development is administered by a management company and owners must be 60 or over.Service includes an on-site warden, community alarm service, use of a residents' lounge in the Manor House and on site functions.ServicesMains water, electricity and drainage. Night storage heating.Local authority/council taxSouth Hams District Council Council tax band C.DirectionsEntrance is opposite Morrisons Supermarket.What3Words : Turkeys.majoring.buffoonviewingsViewing strictly by appointment with Luscombe Maye | lettingsLuscombe Maye also offers an Award Winning Lettings service. If you are considering Letting your own property, or a buy to let purchase, please contact Andrew or Ross on or to discuss our range of bespoke services. A particular feature of the property is the spacious kitchen/dining room, which offers ample space for cooking, dining and everyday living. Its practical layout is well suited to modern lifestyles.The accommodation comprises two bedrooms, including a well-proportioned double bedroom and a versatile second bedroom, ideal as a study, nursery or guest room. A family bathroom completes the interior and is fitted with a bath and overhead shower, WC and hand basin.Externally, the property enjoys a private gravelled and patio area, providing an attractive outdoor space for relaxing and entertaining.Conveniently located within easy reach of Totnes town centre, this apartment would make an ideal first-time purchase, investment opportunity or downsize home.Totnes The unique and charming market town of Totnes sits above the River Dart in the heart of the South Hams. properties.title: 3 bed property for sale
properties.fullDescription: Description Luscombe Maye are delighted to bring to market a characterful three bedroom maisonette, ideally located in the heart of vibrant Totnes town offering the excellent opportunity to enjoy town centre living, while having the amazing benefit of zone permit parking.Arranged over two floors, the property provides generous and versatile accommodation. Upon entering the property you are welcomed by a practical entrance hall leading into a bright and inviting living area. This spacious room provides ample space for both relaxation and entertaining while large windows flood the space with natural light. Adjacent to the living room is the large kitchen-diner, fitted with wall and base units offering plenty of space for a family dining table, perfect for everyday living and hosting guests. The room benefits from the large sash windows that allow plentiful of light to fill the room, making it a very comfortable place. Also on the ground floor is a substantial double room, offering flexibility as a guest room, home office or hobby space. This room is further enhanced by a charming mezzanine level, providing useful additional space and character.A striking spiral staircase leads to the upper floor, where you will find two further generously sized double bedrooms. Both rooms feature large windows, allowing natural light to pour in and creating bright, airy spaces. The master bedroom also benefits from fitted storage. Completing the accommodation is a family bathroom, fitted with a bath and shower above, WC and hand basin.Totnes The unique and charming market town of Totnes sits above the River Dart in the heart of the South Hams. The town is said to have more listed buildings per head of the population than anywhere else in Britain. It is the second oldest borough in England, with an ancient Guildhall and Church, a Norman Castle and its North and East Gates. properties.title: 2 bed property for sale
properties.fullDescription: Description Luscombe Maye are delighted to bring to market a well-proportioned two bedroom apartment situated in a convenient central Totnes location. This property offers bright and practical accommodation. Rotherfold is ideally positioned close to the town centre, with a range of restaurants, shops and transport links close at hand.This property is accessed via a communal entrance and opens into a central hallway which provides access to all rooms. The reception room is a generously sized and light-filled space, featuring high ceilings and sash-style windows that allows natural light to flood the room, creating an airy and welcoming living area ideal for both relaxing and entertaining.Adjacent to this is the separate kitchen which is neatly arranged and fitted with a range of units and appliances, with ample work surface space. This room is equally as light, making it a pleasant and functional workspace.There are two bedrooms, including a spacious master double bedroom and a further well-sized single bedroom, both offering a versatile space as sleeping accommodation, a home office or guest room. The bathroom is centrally located and fitted with a bath with shower above, handbasin and WC.The Rotherfold was originally the local cattle market. The Bull Inn provided stabling on the site of our building, with a hay loft on the upper floor where the local children used to play and also drop hay down to the horses. As motor vehicles became more common, the stables were demolished and replaced by a garage, with a petrol pump on the forecourt. The garage was replaced by the current building in approximately 1987.Totnes The unique and charming market town of Totnes sits above the River Dart in the heart of the South Hams. The town is one of Devon's gems, the birthplace of the Transition Town movement in England and nominated in British Airways' HighLife magazine as one of the world's top ten funky towns. Full of colour and character, its atmosphere is cosmopolitan with a West Country flavour and those looking for history, alternative therapies and beautiful countryside will find it in and around Totnes. The town is said to have more listed buildings per head of the population than anywhere else in Britain. With a twice-a-week market and full of small independent shops, it's well-known for ethical products, wholefoods and fair trade goods. It is the second oldest borough in England, with an ancient Guildhall and Church, a Norman Castle and its North and East Gates. There is a well-regarded community college and a mainline railway station and Dartmoor, the South Hams and many of its fine beaches are within easy reach.Further information To ensure legal compliance, we require our sellers to complete a Property Information Questionnaire or provide a Material Information Guide along with the title document. properties.title: 2 bed property for sale
properties.description: with Views and GardenListed on 27th Feb 2026
properties.fullDescription: This luxury apartment which makes up the whole of the ground floor of a detached early Victorian Villa has been recently refurbished to a high standard. In an elevated position above Moat Hill it offers quiet seclusion yet it is within easy walking distance of the town centre. Our clients have secured a rented parking space about 5 minutes walk away (£450 pa.)Steps lead up to a private pathway to the part glazed front door which opens into the entrance hall with space for outdoor coats and shoes. This leads directly into the attractive kitchen/dining room which is the heart of the property and is the perfect entertaining space, with a lovely wood burner to make it cosy on those winter evenings. The kitchen has bespoke floor and wall mounted cabinets with integrated fridge-freezer and larder cupboard. There is a butlers sink with mixer taps and Calacatta Quartz work surfaces which gives the kitchen a high end feel. There is space for a dining table to seat at least six. Glazed doors lead out to a sun room which in turn accesses the garden.From the kitchen a small hallway with a large storage cupboard gives access to the double aspect living room. With wood floors it has a feature Victorian fireplace with tiled surround and open fire. With views onto the garden this is a light and airy room and has fitted cupboards that cleverly hide the TV. The master bedroom is also at this end of the apartment and is a large double with a Victorian fireplace and walk in wardrobe. Off it is the luxury ensuite shower room with large walk in shower. The second double bedroom is on the other side of the property and has glazed French doors opening onto a private terrace and a further window overlooking the garden. The luxurious family bathroom is next door and has a feature freestanding Arcade bath, marble surround basin, storage cupboard and shelving. A window overlooks the rear terrace.OutsideThe large secluded rear garden has beautiful Devon stone walls on three sides and is mainly laid to lawn with mature shrubs and trees. There are fantastic views over Totnes and beyond and several seating areas to take advantage of the sun throughout the day. There is also a useful and attractive garden shed. To the rear of the property is a private terrace with an outside shower. From here steps lead up to a raised area which would lend itself well to raised vegetable beds or provide a lovely seating area offering more views over Totnes.On street parking can be found close by.Planning PermissionThe current owners have obtained planning permission to extend the property by demolishing the sun room to create a new kitchen dining room and therefore make the property a 3 bedroom apartment. South Hams Planning no. 2150/23/fulLocationMoat Hill House is approx. 5 minutes’ walk into Totnes Town Centre. Set above a single track road it is a quiet, tucked away location yet convenient for all amenities. A short stroll away is the entrance to the Sharpham Estate which gives access to a l

properties.description: this Lovely 2 Bedroom Apartment with ParkingListed on 12th Mar 2026
properties.fullDescription: The apartment is on the ground floor of this lovely Victorian property and is a short walk to Totnes town centre. With amazing panoramic views over Totnes, the River Dart and the surrounding countryside. The property has had a new roof and has been painted recently.Set in an elevated position it is approached along a private road and up a driveway where you have parking and a large single garage for this property.A path by the side of the house with useful storage cupboard leads to a part glazed front door into an inner hallway with space for hanging coats and you will find the WC here. Doors lead from the hallway to both bedrooms the bathroom and the living room.The living room is full of character with high ceilings and there is a beautiful feature Victorian fireplace with log burner, perfect for those chilly evenings. A floor to ceiling French window with doors onto the private garden offers views over Totnes and floods the room with light.An open doorway leads into the double aspect kitchen which has both base and wall units with worksurfaces and single drainer stainless sink and an open hatch to the living room. There is a free standing gas oven with 4 gas burners and space for fridge/freezer and washing machine. Here you will find the new Worcester gas boiler.Bedroom one is very spacious and has a very large sash window with bedroom 2 being also a double. The family bathroom has a P shape bath, hand basin and medicine cabinet.OutsideDirectly accessed from the living room is the front garden which is laid to lawn and has mature flower beds. A seating area with stunning views to the South, East and North over Totnes and beyond is the perfect place for al fresco dining. Part of the garden is gravelled to allow for parking for one carGarage 6.7m x 3.1m (21ft x 10ft)Slightly up from the apartment on the tarmac driveway there is a large garage, great for keeping a car and/or for storage. A door leads out to Totnes Down Hill.SituationSituated on the edge of Totnes Dart Vilas is a series of grand Victorian villas in a select, quiet area about a 5 minute walk to the centre of Totnes. The lovely walk along the river on the Sharpham Estate is a few minutes walk away. Totnes is a medieval market town which is surrounded by the beautiful rolling countryside of the South Hams. Located at the navigable head of the River Dart this unique town has a worldwide reputation for its lively, diverse community and relaxed atmosphere. There are a plethora of restaurants, bars and pubs as well as many privately owned shops and art galleries.Totnes main line Train station is approx. Half a mile walk away with its direct connection to London Paddington (approx. 2hr 45mins) Exeter St David’s (approx. 35 mins) and Plymouth (approx. 30 mins). Exeter airport is 30 miles away (approx. 40 mins) giving direct flights to London, UK and Europe. The A38 giving access to the motorway network is about a 10 minute drive.DirectionsFrom The Plains going away from the Seven Stars tur

Large windows bring in plenty of natural light, creating a warm and inviting atmosphere throughout.One of the standout features is the stunning private garden - a true sanctuary.
